blocks.text: Class Diagram for University Timetable Optimization and Management System

rows: Develop a detailed class diagram: classes, attributes, methods, multiplicities

blocks.text: The table below lists every class with its layer, key attributes, and key methods.

rows: name: String, startDate: Date, endDate: Date
code: String, title: String, creditHours: Integer
name, program, semester, batch, size: Integer
weeklySessions: Integer, requiredRoomType: String, sessionDuration: Integer
name, status: TimetableStatus, qualityScore: Float, hardConflictCount: Integer, unplacedSessionCount: Integer, createdAt: DateTime
generate(), publish(), getConflicts(), getQualityReport(), compareTo(), export(format)
sessionNumber: Integer, status: SessionStatus, locked: Boolean
day: DayOfWeek, startTime: Time, endTime: Time, isMorning: Boolean, isLastSlot: Boolean
name, policyType, priority: Integer, weight: Float, isHardConstraint: Boolean, isEnabled: Boolean
apply(session): Boolean, getPenalty(session): Float, validate(): Boolean
requestType, reason, urgency, status: RequestStatus, createdAt: DateTime
name: String, date: Date, reason: String
code, floor: Integer, capacity: Integer, roomType: RoomType, maintenanceStatus: Boolean
versionNumber: Integer, createdAt: DateTime, changeSummary: String
message: String, notificationType, createdAt: DateTime, read: Boolean
entityType, action, oldValue, newValue: String, timestamp: DateTime, performedBy: String

blocks.text: The following table documents every relationship in the class diagram with its type and multiplicity.

rows: Person → Teacher, Student, TimetableAdministrator, DeptCoordinator, FacilityManager, SystemAdministrator
Person is abstract base; login/logout common to all
University has at least one Faculty
Faculty has at least one Department
Department employs zero or more Teachers
Department has its own timetables per term
Offerings are scoped to a term
Term may have zero or more holidays
Multiple timetable drafts per term allowed
Teacher may be assigned to multiple offerings
Teacher may declare availability for many timeslots
Each availability record covers exactly one timeslot
Sessions exist only within a timetable
At least one session per offering per week
0..1 allows unplaced sessions without a timeslot
0..1 allows unplaced sessions without a room
Timetable is guided by active policies
Every saved timetable has at least one version
Any person can submit change requests
Admin actions are recorded in audit log

blocks.text: The following enumerations are used as attribute types throughout the class diagram.

rows: Centralises identity (name, email, login/logout) and avoids duplication across six actor types. All role-specific classes simply extend Person.
Allows unplaced sessions to exist as valid objects inside a draft timetable, supporting partial generation and conflict reporting.
Course is a reusable description; CourseOffering ties it to a specific term, teacher, and section. Same course can be offered multiple times across terms without duplication.
ClassSession only makes sense inside a Timetable. Composition signals that sessions are deleted if the timetable is removed.
Supports UC15 (Compare Timetable Versions) and provides an audit trail for timetable changes over time.
Decouples logging from domain classes. Any entity change can be logged without modifying the entity itself.
Unifies hard and soft constraint configuration into one class, matching the constraint screen in the UI. isHardConstraint determines enforcement vs. penalisation.
TimetableStatus, SessionStatus, RoomType etc. prevent invalid attribute values and make the design implementation-ready.

blocks.text: The full interactive class diagram is included in the interactive HTML submission and in the figure below. It is organised in six horizontal bands:
Roles and people — Person hierarchy at the top
Academic structure — University, Faculty, Department, AcademicTerm, Course, StudentSection
Scheduling core — CourseOffering, Timetable, ClassSession, Timeslot, TeacherAvailability
System-wide — ChangeRequest, Notification, TimetableVersion, AuditLog, enumerations
